nodes.context: Armed Chinese robbers and bandits operating in eastern Russia-China borderland, southeastern Siberia, Russian Far East, and Manchuria during late 19th to early 20th century.
nodes.time_period: second half of 19th century - first half of 20th century

nodes.context: Chinese anti-foreign, anti-colonial militia that participated in the Boxer Rebellion against the Eight-Nation Alliance.
The army of the Empire of Japan, which recruited Honghuzi as guerrillas during the Russo-Japanese War.
A large army of Honghuzi commanded by Zhang Zuolin, allied with the Japanese during the Russo-Japanese War.
A specific band of Honghuzi led by Chung Tsor Lin, later incorporated into Chinese army.

edges.context: Honghuzi were also Boxers and participated together in the Boxer Rebellion against the Eight-Nation Alliance.
Groups of Honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War.
Zhang Zuolin commanded a large army of Honghuzi, who were allied to the Japanese during the war.
Zhang Zuolin's Honghuzi forces were allied with the Japanese during the Russo-Japanese War.
Chung Tsor Lin's band is a subgroup of the Honghuzi.
